title: TC001-Submit route search with valid pickup and destination navigates to results with query params
description: Verifies that completing both fields and submitting the search navigates to the route comparison results page with origin and destination reflected in the URL.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type 'Blok M' into pickup (index 3), type 'Stasiun Sudirman' into destination (index 4), then click 'Cari Rute Terbaik' (index 94). After that, verify the URL contains '/routes', 'origin=' and 'destination='.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Blok M')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Stasiun Sudirman')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Validation: missing pickup shows inline error and stays on home page
description: Verifies the form prevents submission when pickup is empty and shows an inline validation message for Lokasi Penjemputan.
code:

ASSERTION: text 'Lokasi Penjemputan' is visible on the page. ASSERTION: text 'wajib' is visible (inline validation). After verifying these, finish and report results.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent

        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"

    finally:

            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC020-Email/password Masuk shows not-wired/error behavior without completing end-to-end login
description: Verifies that attempting email/password sign-in does not complete login and instead shows an error/notice or remains on the login page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login by using the navigate action to 'http://localhost:3000/login'.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
        
        # -> Type 'user@example.com' into Email, type 'wrongpassword' into Password, then click 'Masuk'. After click, verify URL contains '/login' and that text 'Masuk' is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('user@example.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('wrongpassword')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type 'Jakarta, Selatan' into the 'Lokasi Penjemputan' input (index 3).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Jakarta, Selatan')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Tanah Abang, Jakarta Pusat')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div[2]/div[3]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC010-Results page handles missing query parameters gracefully

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None

    context = None

            headless=True,

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Click the 'Rute' button/link (interactive element index 44) to open the results (/routes) page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        # -> Navigate to /routes using the explicit navigation action so the results page can be inspected for the empty/error state.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/routes")
        
        # -> Click the 'Ubah Pencarian' button (interactive element index 422) to return to the search page so the URL can be verified.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/div/div[3]/a/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent

    finally:
        if context:

        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
